source: USDA, Census Bureau, BLS, NerdWallet, Bankrate, Investopedia

birthWithInsurance.note: Vaginal delivery with insurance. C-section adds $5K-10K

babyGearFirstYear.note: Crib, car seat, stroller, clothes, diapers setup

childcare.note: Varies enormously by location. Highest in NE and West Coast

annualCostUSDA.note: USDA estimates $18,271/year average for middle-income families

initialRepairs.note: Things you want to fix before moving in

mortgagePayment.formula: standard amortization based on price, down payment, rate, term

homeInsurance.note: Varies by location, coverage, home value

pmi.note: Only if down payment < 20%. Typically 0.5-1% of loan annually

maintenance.note: Budget 1-2% of home value per year for maintenance

hoa.note: Not all homes have HOA. Condos almost always do.

utilities.note: Often higher than apartment due to more space

taxBenefits.mortgageInterestDeduction: Deduct interest on first $750K of mortgage debt
taxBenefits.propertyTaxDeduction: Deduct up to $10,000 in state/local taxes (SALT cap)
taxBenefits.capitalGainsExclusion: Exclude $250K ($500K married) in gains when selling after 2+ years

securityDeposit.note: First + last month + security. May overlap with old lease.

leaseBreakFee.note: Typically 1-2 months rent if breaking early

temporaryHousing.note: If you need a hotel/Airbnb while finding housing

ongoingImpacts.transportationDifference: Car costs vary by city (insurance, gas, parking)
ongoingImpacts.salaryAdjustment: Employers often adjust salary for cost of living

ongoingImpacts.combinedIncome: Dual income household — often significant increase
ongoingImpacts.sharedExpenses: Housing, utilities, insurance split = lower per-person cost
ongoingImpacts.taxImpact: Marriage can increase or decrease taxes depending on income disparity
ongoingImpacts.insuranceSavings: Combined auto, health, and home insurance often cheaper
ongoingImpacts.estatePlanning: Need updated beneficiaries, wills, possibly trusts

incomeGap.note: Lost income during transition. Budget 3-6 months.

ongoingImpacts.salaryChange: Research median salary in new field vs current
ongoingImpacts.benefitsChange: 401k match, health insurance, PTO may differ significantly
ongoingImpacts.growthTrajectory: New field may have faster or slower salary growth curve
ongoingImpacts.satisfaction: Career satisfaction affects spending patterns (less retail therapy, fewer stress purchases)

healthInsurance.note: Must purchase individually. ACA marketplace or private.

ongoingImpacts.retirementContributions: No employer match. Must self-fund SEP IRA or Solo 401k.
ongoingImpacts.incomeVariability: Income becomes unpredictable. Budget based on worst-case months.
ongoingImpacts.taxDeductions: Business expenses are tax deductible: home office, equipment, travel, meals (50%)

recommendations.emergencyFund: Have 6-12 months personal expenses saved before starting
recommendations.keepDayJob: Consider starting as a side business first
recommendations.separateAccounts: Business and personal finances must be completely separate

incomeReplacement.fourPercentRule: Withdraw 4% of portfolio annually for ~30-year retirement
incomeReplacement.requiredMinimumDistributions: Must start withdrawing from 401k/Traditional IRA at age 73

healthcareBefore65.note: No Medicare until 65. ACA marketplace or COBRA.

longTermCare.note: 50% of people over 65 will need some form

expenses.generalExpenses: Typically 70-80% of pre-retirement expenses. Travel may increase, commuting drops.
